para: Read-only support for GlobalSat DG-388 GPS data logger .gpl binary track log files.

The DG-388 is a USB mass-storage GPS data logger built around the Airoha AG3335M multi-GNSS chipset. It records track logs to its internal flash memory as .gpl files containing fixed 28-byte binary records with no file header.

Each record stores date, time (UTC), latitude, longitude, altitude, speed, heading, and a record-type flag. The record-type flag distinguishes automatic track log entries (flag=2) from manual waypoints recorded by pressing the device's button (flag=3). Manual waypoints have no name field; GPSBabel assigns sequential names (WPT-001, WPT-002, etc.).

example.title: Command showing DG-388 .gpl to GPX conversion

para: The DG-388 presents its flash storage as a standard USB mass-storage device, so .gpl files can be copied directly. The device can also output live NMEA sentences at 4800 baud via its USB-serial interface.
